name: DEC PDP-1 — MACRO assembly instructions
source: Compiled from the PDP-1 instruction set (MACRO mnemonics; cf. the DEC PDP-1 manuals and the spacewar1962.github.io reference).
note: Lookups match the leading mnemonic of a token (case-insensitive). Operate/skip/shift micro-instructions are listed too.

entries.and: Logical AND — AC ← AC ∧ C(Y). Bitwise AND of the accumulator with memory word Y.
entries.ior: Inclusive OR — AC ← AC ∨ C(Y). Bitwise inclusive-or with memory word Y.
entries.xor: Exclusive OR — AC ← AC ⊕ C(Y). Bitwise exclusive-or with memory word Y.
entries.xct: Execute — execute the instruction held in memory word Y, in place, without branching.
entries.lac: Load Accumulator — AC ← C(Y). Copy memory word Y into the accumulator.
entries.lio: Load In-Out register — IO ← C(Y). Copy memory word Y into the IO register.
entries.dac: Deposit Accumulator — C(Y) ← AC. Store the accumulator into memory word Y.
entries.dap: Deposit Address Part — store AC bits 6–17 (the address field) into Y, leaving Y's opcode intact. Used for self-modifying address patches.
entries.dip: Deposit Instruction Part — store AC bits 0–5 (the opcode field) into Y, leaving Y's address intact.
entries.dio: Deposit In-Out register — C(Y) ← IO. Store the IO register into memory word Y.
entries.dzm: Deposit Zero in Memory — C(Y) ← 0. Clear memory word Y.
entries.add: Add — AC ← AC + C(Y), ones-complement, setting Overflow on carry out of the sign.
entries.idx: Index — AC ← C(Y) + 1, and store back: C(Y) ← AC. Increment memory word Y.
entries.isp: Index and Skip if Positive — increment C(Y); skip the next instruction if the result is positive (≥ 0).
entries.sad: Skip if Accumulator and memory Differ — skip the next instruction if AC ≠ C(Y).
entries.sas: Skip if Accumulator and memory Same — skip the next instruction if AC = C(Y).
entries.mus: Multiply Step — one step of the multiply subroutine (the PDP-1 had no hardware multiply by default).
entries.dis: Divide Step — one step of the divide subroutine.
entries.jmp: Jump — transfer control to address Y (PC ← Y).
entries.jsp: Jump and Save Program counter — AC ← return address, then PC ← Y. The classic subroutine call.
entries.cal: Call subroutine — deposit the return address at register 100 (octal) and jump to 101. Fixed-address subroutine linkage.
entries.jda: Jump and Deposit Accumulator — store AC at Y, then jump to Y+1. Subroutine call that also passes the accumulator.
entries.law: Load Accumulator With — AC ← the literal N in the address field (immediate load). 'law i' loads the ones-complement (negative) literal.
entries.iot: In-Out Transfer — initiate or complete a transfer with an IO device; the device is selected by the address/IO bits.
entries.opr: Operate — the micro-coded group (clear, complement, halt, no-op, set/clear flags) selected by bits in the address field.
entries.skp: Skip group — conditional-skip micro-instructions selected by bits in the address field (sza, spa, sma, szo, spi, szs, szf…). 'i' inverts the sense.
entries.sft: Shift group — the shift/rotate micro-instructions (ral, rar, rcl, rcr, sal, sar, scl, scr, ril, rir, sil, sir); the count is a unary mask in the address field.
entries.nop: No Operation — do nothing for one cycle ('opr' with no bits set).
entries.stf: Set Flag — set program flag f (1–6).
entries.clf: Clear Flag — clear program flag f (1–6).
entries.sza: Skip if Zero Accumulator — skip if AC = 0.
entries.spa: Skip if Plus Accumulator — skip if AC sign is positive (bit 0 = 0).
entries.sma: Skip if Minus Accumulator — skip if AC sign is negative (bit 0 = 1).
entries.szo: Skip if Zero Overflow — skip if the overflow flip-flop is clear (and clear it).
entries.spi: Skip if Plus In-Out register — skip if the IO register's sign is positive.
entries.szs: Skip if Zero Switches — skip if the selected sense switches are zero.
entries.szf: Skip if Zero Flag — skip if the selected program flag is zero.
entries.ral: Rotate Accumulator Left — circular left shift of AC.
entries.rar: Rotate Accumulator Right — circular right shift of AC.
entries.rcl: Rotate Combined Left — circular left shift of the 36-bit AC+IO pair.
entries.rcr: Rotate Combined Right — circular right shift of the AC+IO pair.
entries.ril: Rotate In-Out Left — circular left shift of IO.
entries.rir: Rotate In-Out Right — circular right shift of IO.
entries.sal: Shift Accumulator Left — arithmetic (sign-preserving) left shift of AC.
entries.sar: Shift Accumulator Right — arithmetic right shift of AC.
entries.scl: Shift Combined Left — arithmetic left shift of the AC+IO pair.
entries.scr: Shift Combined Right — arithmetic right shift of the AC+IO pair.
entries.sil: Shift In-Out Left — arithmetic left shift of IO.
entries.sir: Shift In-Out Right — arithmetic right shift of IO.
entries.tyo: Type Out — send the character in IO to the typewriter (IOT device).
entries.tyi: Type In — read a typed character into IO (IOT device).
entries.rpa: Read Paper-tape Alphanumeric — read a line of paper tape into IO (IOT).
entries.rpb: Read Paper-tape Binary — read binary paper tape into IO (IOT).
entries.ppa: Punch Paper-tape Alphanumeric — punch IO to paper tape (IOT).
entries.ppb: Punch Paper-tape Binary — punch IO in binary to paper tape (IOT).
entries.dpy: Display — plot a point on the Type 30 CRT at the coordinates in AC and IO. The instruction that drew Spacewar!.
entries.cks: Check Status — read device status flags into IO (IOT).
entries.macro: MACRO directive — begin a macro-instruction definition.
entries.define: MACRO/MIDAS directive — define a macro (the body follows until 'term').
entries.term: MACRO directive — terminate (end) the current macro definition.
entries.repeat: MACRO directive — repeat the following text/instruction n times.
entries.constants: MACRO directive — emit the pool of accumulated literal constants here.
entries.variables: MACRO directive — reserve storage for the declared variables here.
entries.start: MACRO directive — mark the program's entry point (start address).
